title: trisetaria cavanillesii
intro: Trisetaria cavanillesii, also known as Spanish needle grass, is a species of grass native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving congestion. In herbal preparations, Trisetaria cavanillesii is often combined with other plants to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to aid in the relief of respiratory symptoms.
